I arise, and they speak against me.    </span>          <span class="versetext" id="job19-19" style="display: inline;"><span class="versenum">19</span>    All my close friends abhor me, And those whom I love have turned against me.    </span>          <span class="versetext" id="job19-20" style="display: inline;"><span class="versenum">20</span>    My bone clings to my skin and to my flesh, And I have escaped by the skin of my teeth.    </span>          <span class="versetext" id="job19-21" style="display: inline;"><span class="versenum">21</span>    "Have pity on me, have pity on me, O you my friends, For the hand of God has struck me!    </span>          <span class="versetext" id="job19-22" style="display: inline;"><span class="versenum">22</span>    Why do you persecute me as God does, And are not satisfied with my flesh?    </span>          <span class="versetext" id="job19-23" style="display: inline;"><span class="versenum">23</span>    "Oh, that my words were written! Oh, that they were inscribed in a book!    </span>          <span class="versetext" id="job19-24" style="display: inline;"><span class="versenum">24</span>    That they were engraved on a rock With an iron pen and lead, forever!    </span>          <span class="versetext" id="job19-25" style="display: inline;"><span class="versenum">25</span>    For I know that my Redeemer lives, And He shall stand at last on the earth;    </span>          <span class="versetext" id="job19-26" style="display: inline;"><span class="versenum">26</span>    And after my skin is destroyed, this I know, That in my flesh I shall see God,    </span>          <span class="versetext" id="job19-27" style="display: inline;"><span class="versenum">27</span>    Whom I shall see for myself, And my eyes shall behold, and not another. How my heart yearns within me!    </span>          <span class="versetext" id="job19-28" style="display: inline;"><span class="versenum">28</span>    If you should say, 'How shall we persecute him?'-- Since the root of the matter is found in me,    </span>          <span class="versetext" id="job19-29" style="display: inline;"><span class="versenum">29</span>    Be afraid of the sword for yourselves; For wrath brings the punishment of the sword, That you may know there is a judgment."    </span><br /><i>Twenty One Jobs Replies to Zophars Second Speech</i><br /><span class="versetext" id="job21-1" style="display: inline;"><span class="versenum">1</span>    Then Job answered and said:    </span>          <span class="versetext" id="job21-2" style="display: inline;"><span class="versenum">2</span>    "Listen carefully to my speech, And let this be your consolation.    </span>          <span class="versetext" id="job21-3" style="display: inline;"><span class="versenum">3</span>    Bear with me that I may speak, And after I have spoken, keep mocking.    </span>          <span class="versetext" id="job21-4" style="display: inline;"><span class="versenum">4</span>    "As for me, is my complaint against man? And if it were, why should I not be impatient?    </span>          <span class="versetext" id="job21-5" style="display: inline;"><span class="versenum">5</span>    Look at me and be astonished; Put your hand over your mouth.    </span>          <span class="versetext" id="job21-6" style="display: inline;"><span class="versenum">6</span>    Even when I remember I am terrified, And trembling takes hold of my flesh.    </span>          <span class="versetext" id="job21-7" style="display: inline;"><span class="versenum">7</span>    Why do the wicked live and become old, Yes, become mighty in power?    </span>          <span class="versetext" id="job21-8" style="display: inline;"><span class="versenum">8</span>    Their descendants are established with them in their sight, And their offspring before their eyes.    </span>          <span class="versetext" id="job21-9" style="display: inline;"><span class="versenum">9</span>    Their houses are safe from fear, Neither is the rod of God upon them.    </span>          <span class="versetext" id="job21-10" style="display: inline;"><span class="versenum">10</span>    Their bull breeds without failure; Their cow calves without miscarriage.    </span>          <span class="versetext" id="job21-11" style="display: inline;"><span class="versenum">11</span>    They send forth their little ones like a flock, And their children dance.    </span>          <span class="versetext" id="job21-12" style="display: inline;"><span class="versenum">12</span>    They sing to the tambourine and harp, And rejoice to the sound of the flute.    </span>          <span class="versetext" id="job21-13" style="display: inline;"><span class="versenum">13</span>    They spend their days in wealth, And in a moment go down to the grave. <a href="http://draft.blogger.com/null" name="a"></a>   </span>          <span class="versetext" id="job21-14" style="display: inline;"><span class="versenum">14</span>    Yet they say to God, 'Depart from us, For we do not desire the knowledge of Your ways.    </span>          <span class="versetext" id="job21-15" style="display: inline;"><span class="versenum">15</span>    Who is the Almighty, that we should serve Him? And what profit do we have if we pray to Him?'    </span>          <span class="versetext" id="job21-16" style="display: inline;"><span class="versenum">16</span>    Indeed their prosperity is not in their hand; The counsel of the wicked is far from me.    </span>          <span class="versetext" id="job21-17" style="display: inline;"><span class="versenum">17</span>    "How often is the lamp of the wicked put out? How often does their  destruction come upon them, The sorrows God distributes in His anger?    </span>          <span class="versetext" id="job21-18" style="display: inline;"><span class="versenum">18</span>    They are like straw before the wind, And like chaff that a storm carries away.    </span>          <span class="versetext" id="job21-19" style="display: inline;"><span class="versenum">19</span>    They say, 'God lays up one's <a href="http://draft.blogger.com/null" name="b"></a> iniquity for his children'; Let Him recompense him, that he may know it.    </span>          <span class="versetext" id="job21-20" style="display: inline;"><span class="versenum">20</span>    Let his eyes see his destruction, And let him drink of the wrath of the Almighty.    </span>          <span class="versetext" id="job21-21" style="display: inline;"><span class="versenum">21</span>    For what does he care about his household after him, When the number of his months is cut in half?    </span>          <span class="versetext" id="job21-22" style="display: inline;"><span class="versenum">22</span>    "Can anyone teach God knowledge, Since He judges those on high?    </span>          <span class="versetext" id="job21-23" style="display: inline;"><span class="versenum">23</span>    One dies in his full strength, Being wholly at ease and secure;    </span>          <span class="versetext" id="job21-24" style="display: inline;"><span class="versenum">24</span>    His pails <a href="http://draft.blogger.com/null" name="c"></a> are full of milk, And the marrow of his bones is moist.    </span>          <span class="versetext" id="job21-25" style="display: inline;"><span class="versenum">25</span>    Another man dies in the bitterness of his soul, Never having eaten with pleasure.    </span>          <span class="versetext" id="job21-26" style="display: inline;"><span class="versenum">26</span>    They lie down alike in the dust, And worms cover them.    </span>          <span class="versetext" id="job21-27" style="display: inline;"><span class="versenum">27</span>    "Look, I know your thoughts, And the schemes with which you would wrong me.    </span>          <span class="versetext" id="job21-28" style="display: inline;"><span class="versenum">28</span>    For you say, 'Where is the house of the prince? And where is the tent, <a href="http://draft.blogger.com/null" name="d"></a> The dwelling place of the wicked?'    </span>          <span class="versetext" id="job21-29" style="display: inline;"><span class="versenum">29</span>    Have you not asked those who travel the road? And do you not know their signs?    </span>          <span class="versetext" id="job21-30" style="display: inline;"><span class="versenum">30</span>    For the wicked are reserved for the day of doom; They shall be brought out on the day of wrath.    </span>          <span class="versetext" id="job21-31" style="display: inline;"><span class="versenum">31</span>    Who condemns his way to his face? And who repays him for what he has done?    </span>          <span class="versetext" id="job21-32" style="display: inline;"><span class="versenum">32</span>    Yet he shall be brought to the grave, And a vigil kept over the tomb.    </span>          <span class="versetext" id="job21-33" style="display: inline;"><span class="versenum">33</span>    The clods of the valley shall be sweet to him; Everyone shall follow him, As countless have gone before him.    </span>          <span class="versetext" id="job21-34" style="display: inline;"><span class="versenum">34</span>    How then can you comfort me with empty words, Since falsehood remains in your answers?"    </span><br /><i>Twenty Three Job Replies to Eliphaz's Third Speech</i><br /><span class="versetext" id="job23-1" style="display: inline;"><span class="versenum">1</span>    Then Job answered and said:    </span>          <span class="versetext" id="job23-2" style="display: inline;"><span class="versenum">2</span>    "Even today my complaint is bitter; My <a href="http://draft.blogger.com/null" name="a"></a> hand is listless because of my groaning.    </span>          <span class="versetext" id="job23-3" style="display: inline;"><span class="versenum">3</span>    Oh, that I knew where I might find Him, That I might come to His seat!    </span>          <span class="versetext" id="job23-4" style="display: inline;"><span class="versenum">4</span>    I would present my case before Him, And fill my mouth with arguments.    </span>          <span class="versetext" id="job23-5" style="display: inline;"><span class="versenum">5</span>    I would know the words which He would answer me, And understand what He would say to me.    </span>          <span class="versetext" id="job23-6" style="display: inline;"><span class="versenum">6</span>    Would He contend with me in His great power? No! But He would take note of me.    </span>          <span class="versetext" id="job23-7" style="display: inline;"><span class="versenum">7</span>    There the upright could reason with Him, And I would be delivered forever from my Judge.    </span>          <span class="versetext" id="job23-8" style="display: inline;"><span class="versenum">8</span>    "Look, I go forward, but He is not there, And backward, but I cannot perceive Him;    </span>          <span class="versetext" id="job23-9" style="display: inline;"><span class="versenum">9</span>    When He works on the left hand, I cannot behold Him; When He turns to the right hand, I cannot see Him.    </span>          <span class="versetext" id="job23-10" style="display: inline;"><span class="versenum">10</span>    But He knows the way that I take; When He has tested me, I shall come forth as gold.    </span>          <span class="versetext" id="job23-11" style="display: inline;"><span class="versenum">11</span>    My foot has held fast to His steps; I have kept His way and not turned aside.    </span>          <span class="versetext" id="job23-12" style="display: inline;"><span class="versenum">12</span>    I have not departed from the commandment of His lips; I have treasured the words of His mouth More than my necessary food.    </span>          <span class="versetext" id="job23-13" style="display: inline;"><span class="versenum">13</span>    "But He is unique, and who can make Him change? And whatever His soul desires, that He does.    </span>          <span class="versetext" id="job23-14" style="display: inline;"><span class="versenum">14</span>    For He performs what is appointed for me, And many such things are with Him.    </span>          <span class="versetext" id="job23-15" style="display: inline;"><span class="versenum">15</span>    Therefore I am terrified at His presence; When I consider this, I am afraid of Him.    </span>          <span class="versetext" id="job23-16" style="display: inline;"><span class="versenum">16</span>    For God made my heart weak, And the Almighty terrifies me;    </span>          <span class="versetext" id="job23-17" style="display: inline;"><span class="versenum">17</span>    Because I was not cut off from the presence of darkness, And He did not hide deep darkness from my face.    </span><br /><i>Twenty Four Reply to Eliphaz #3 cont...</i><br /><span class="versetext" id="job24-1" style="display: inline;"><span class="versenum">1</span>    "Since times are not hidden from the Almighty, Why do those who know Him see not His days?    </span>          <span class="versetext" id="job24-2" style="display: inline;"><span class="versenum">2</span>    "Some remove landmarks; They seize flocks violently and feed on them;    </span>          <span class="versetext" id="job24-3" style="display: inline;"><span class="versenum">3</span>    They drive away the donkey of the fatherless; They take the widow's ox as a pledge.    </span>          <span class="versetext" id="job24-4" style="display: inline;"><span class="versenum">4</span>    They push the needy off the road; All the poor of the land are forced to hide.    </span>          <span class="versetext" id="job24-5" style="display: inline;"><span class="versenum">5</span>    Indeed, like wild donkeys in the desert, They go out to their work,  searching for food. The wilderness yields food for them and for their  children.    </span>          <span class="versetext" id="job24-6" style="display: inline;"><span class="versenum">6</span>    They gather their fodder in the field And glean in the vineyard of the wicked.    </span>          <span class="versetext" id="job24-7" style="display: inline;"><span class="versenum">7</span>    They spend the night naked, without clothing, And have no covering in the cold.    </span>          <span class="versetext" id="job24-8" style="display: inline;"><span class="versenum">8</span>    They are wet with the showers of the mountains, And huddle around the rock for want of shelter.    </span>          <span class="versetext" id="job24-9" style="display: inline;"><span class="versenum">9</span>    "Some snatch the fatherless from the breast, And take a pledge from the poor.    </span>          <span class="versetext" id="job24-10" style="display: inline;"><span class="versenum">10</span>    They cause the poor to go naked, without clothing; And they take away the sheaves from the hungry.    </span>          <span class="versetext" id="job24-11" style="display: inline;"><span class="versenum">11</span>    They press out oil within their walls, And tread winepresses, yet suffer thirst.    </span>          <span class="versetext" id="job24-12" style="display: inline;"><span class="versenum">12</span>    The dying groan in the city, And the souls of the wounded cry out; Yet God does not charge them with wrong.    </span>          <span class="versetext" id="job24-13" style="display: inline;"><span class="versenum">13</span>    "There are those who rebel against the light; They do not know its ways Nor abide in its paths.    </span>          <span class="versetext" id="job24-14" style="display: inline;"><span class="versenum">14</span>    The murderer rises with the light; He kills the poor and needy; And in the night he is like a thief.    </span>          <span class="versetext" id="job24-15" style="display: inline;"><span class="versenum">15</span>    The eye of the adulterer waits for the twilight, Saying, 'No eye will see me'; And he disguises his face.    </span>          <span class="versetext" id="job24-16" style="display: inline;"><span class="versenum">16</span>    In the dark they break into houses Which they marked for themselves in the daytime; They do not know the light.    </span>          <span class="versetext" id="job24-17" style="display: inline;"><span class="versenum">17</span>    For the morning is the same to them as the shadow of death; If  someone recognizes them, They are in the terrors of the shadow of death.    </span>          <span class="versetext" id="job24-18" style="display: inline;"><span class="versenum">18</span>    "They should be swift on the face of the waters, Their portion  should be cursed in the earth, So that no one would turn into the way of  their vineyards.    </span>          <span class="versetext" id="job24-19" style="display: inline;"><span class="versenum">19</span>    As drought and heat consume the snow waters, So the grave <a href="http://draft.blogger.com/null" name="a"></a> consumes those who have sinned.    </span>          <span class="versetext" id="job24-20" style="display: inline;"><span class="versenum">20</span>    The womb should forget him, The worm should feed sweetly on him; He  should be remembered no more, And wickedness should be broken like a  tree.    </span>          <span class="versetext" id="job24-21" style="display: inline;"><span class="versenum">21</span>    For he preys on the barren who do not bear, And does no good for the widow.    </span>          <span class="versetext" id="job24-22" style="display: inline;"><span class="versenum">22</span>    "But God draws the mighty away with His power; He rises up, but no man is sure of life.    </span>          <span class="versetext" id="job24-23" style="display: inline;"><span class="versenum">23</span>    He gives them security, and they rely on it; Yet His eyes are on their ways.    </span>          <span class="versetext" id="job24-24" style="display: inline;"><span class="versenum">24</span>    They are exalted for a little while, Then they are gone. They are  brought low; They are taken out of the way like all others; They dry out  like the heads of grain.    </span>          <span class="versetext" id="job24-25" style="display: inline;"><span class="versenum">25</span>    "Now if it is not so, who will prove me a liar, And make my speech worth nothing?"    </span><br /><i>Twenty Six Job replies to Bildad's third speech</i><br /><span class="versetext" id="job26-1" style="display: inline;"><span class="versenum">1</span>    But Job answered and said:    </span>          <span class="versetext" id="job26-2" style="display: inline;"><span class="versenum">2</span>    "How have you helped him who is without power? How have you saved the arm that has no strength?    </span>          <span class="versetext" id="job26-3" style="display: inline;"><span class="versenum">3</span>    How have you counseled one who has no wisdom? And how have you declared sound advice to many?    </span>          <span class="versetext" id="job26-4" style="display: inline;"><span class="versenum">4</span>    To whom have you uttered words? And whose spirit came from you?    </span>          <span class="versetext" id="job26-5" style="display: inline;"><span class="versenum">5</span>    "The dead tremble, Those under the waters and those inhabiting them.    </span>          <span class="versetext" id="job26-6" style="display: inline;"><span class="versenum">6</span>    Sheol is naked before Him, And Destruction has no covering.    </span>          <span class="versetext" id="job26-7" style="display: inline;"><span class="versenum">7</span>    He stretches out the north over empty space; He hangs the earth on nothing.    </span>          <span class="versetext" id="job26-8" style="display: inline;"><span class="versenum">8</span>    He binds up the water in His thick clouds, Yet the clouds are not broken under it.    </span>          <span class="versetext" id="job26-9" style="display: inline;"><span class="versenum">9</span>    He covers the face of His throne, And spreads His cloud over it.    </span>          <span class="versetext" id="job26-10" style="display: inline;"><span class="versenum">10</span>    He drew a circular horizon on the face of the waters, At the boundary of light and darkness.    </span>          <span class="versetext" id="job26-11" style="display: inline;"><span class="versenum">11</span>    The pillars of heaven tremble, And are astonished at His rebuke.    </span>          <span class="versetext" id="job26-12" style="display: inline;"><span class="versenum">12</span>    He stirs up the sea with His power, And by His understanding He breaks up the storm.    </span>          <span class="versetext" id="job26-13" style="display: inline;"><span class="versenum">13</span>    By His Spirit He adorned the heavens; His hand pierced the fleeing serpent.    </span>          <span class="versetext" id="job26-14" style="display: inline;"><span class="versenum">14</span>    Indeed these are the mere edges of His ways, And how small a whisper  we hear of Him! But the thunder of His power who can understand?"&nbsp;</span><br /><span class="versetext" id="job26-14" style="display: inline;"><i>Twenty Seven Job Cont... </i></span><br /><span class="versetext" id="job27-1" style="display: inline;"><span class="versenum">1</span>    Moreover Job continued his discourse, and said:    </span>          <span class="versetext" id="job27-2" style="display: inline;"><span class="versenum">2</span>    "As God lives, who has taken away my justice, And the Almighty, who has made my soul bitter,    </span>          <span class="versetext" id="job27-3" style="display: inline;"><span class="versenum">3</span>    As long as my breath is in me, And the breath of God in my nostrils,    </span>          <span class="versetext" id="job27-4" style="display: inline;"><span class="versenum">4</span>    My lips will not speak wickedness, Nor my tongue utter deceit.    </span>          <span class="versetext" id="job27-5" style="display: inline;"><span class="versenum">5</span>    Far be it from me That I should say you are right; Till I die I will not put away my integrity from me.    </span>          <span class="versetext" id="job27-6" style="display: inline;"><span class="versenum">6</span>    My righteousness I hold fast, and will not let it go; My heart shall not reproach me as long as I live.    </span>          <span class="versetext" id="job27-7" style="display: inline;"><span class="versenum">7</span>    "May my enemy be like the wicked, And he who rises up against me like the unrighteous.    </span>          <span class="versetext" id="job27-8" style="display: inline;"><span class="versenum">8</span>    For what is the hope of the hypocrite, Though he may gain much, If God takes away his life?    </span>          <span class="versetext" id="job27-9" style="display: inline;"><span class="versenum">9</span>    Will God hear his cry When trouble comes upon him?    </span>          <span class="versetext" id="job27-10" style="display: inline;"><span class="versenum">10</span>    Will he delight himself in the Almighty? Will he always call on God?    </span>          <span class="versetext" id="job27-11" style="display: inline;"><span class="versenum">11</span>    "I will teach you about the hand of God; What is with the Almighty I will not conceal.    </span>          <span class="versetext" id="job27-12" style="display: inline;"><span class="versenum">12</span>    Surely all of you have seen it; Why then do you behave with complete nonsense?    </span>          <span class="versetext" id="job27-13" style="display: inline;"><span class="versenum">13</span>    "This is the portion of a wicked man with God, And the heritage of oppressors, received from the Almighty:    </span>          <span class="versetext" id="job27-14" style="display: inline;"><span class="versenum">14</span>    If his children are multiplied, it is for the sword; And his offspring shall not be satisfied with bread.    </span>          <span class="versetext" id="job27-15" style="display: inline;"><span class="versenum">15</span>    Those who survive him shall be buried in death, And their <a href="http://draft.blogger.com/null" name="a"></a> widows shall not weep,    </span>          <span class="versetext" id="job27-16" style="display: inline;"><span class="versenum">16</span>    Though he heaps up silver like dust, And piles up clothing like clay--    </span>          <span class="versetext" id="job27-17" style="display: inline;"><span class="versenum">17</span>    He may pile it up, but the just will wear it, And the innocent will divide the silver.    </span>          <span class="versetext" id="job27-18" style="display: inline;"><span class="versenum">18</span>    He builds his house like a moth, <a href="http://draft.blogger.com/null" name="b"></a> Like a booth which a watchman makes.    </span>          <span class="versetext" id="job27-19" style="display: inline;"><span class="versenum">19</span>    The rich man will lie down, But not be gathered up; <a href="http://draft.blogger.com/null" name="c"></a> He opens his eyes, And he is no more.    </span>          <span class="versetext" id="job27-20" style="display: inline;"><span class="versenum">20</span>    Terrors overtake him like a flood; A tempest steals him away in the night.    </span>          <span class="versetext" id="job27-21" style="display: inline;"><span class="versenum">21</span>    The east wind carries him away, and he is gone; It sweeps him out of his place.    </span>          <span class="versetext" id="job27-22" style="display: inline;"><span class="versenum">22</span>    It hurls against him and does not spare; He flees desperately from its power.    </span>          <span class="versetext" id="job27-23" style="display: inline;"><span class="versenum">23</span>    Men shall clap their hands at him, And shall hiss him out of his place.&nbsp;</span><br /><span class="versetext" id="job27-23" style="display: inline;"><i>Twenty Eight Job Cont... </i></span><br /><span class="versetext" id="job28-1" style="display: inline;"><span class="versenum">1</span>    "Surely there is a mine for silver, And a place where gold is refined.    </span>          <span class="versetext" id="job28-2" style="display: inline;"><span class="versenum">2</span>    Iron is taken from the earth, And copper is smelted from ore.    </span>          <span class="versetext" id="job28-3" style="display: inline;"><span class="versenum">3</span>    Man puts an end to darkness, And searches every recess For ore in the darkness and the shadow of death.    </span>          <span class="versetext" id="job28-4" style="display: inline;"><span class="versenum">4</span>    He breaks open a shaft away from people; In places forgotten by feet They hang far away from men; They swing to and fro.    </span>          <span class="versetext" id="job28-5" style="display: inline;"><span class="versenum">5</span>    As for the earth, from it comes bread, But underneath it is turned up as by fire;    </span>          <span class="versetext" id="job28-6" style="display: inline;"><span class="versenum">6</span>    Its stones are the source of sapphires, And it contains gold dust.    </span>          <span class="versetext" id="job28-7" style="display: inline;"><span class="versenum">7</span>    That path no bird knows, Nor has the falcon's eye seen it.    </span>          <span class="versetext" id="job28-8" style="display: inline;"><span class="versenum">8</span>    The proud lions <a href="http://draft.blogger.com/null" name="a"></a> have not trodden it, Nor has the fierce lion passed over it.    </span>          <span class="versetext" id="job28-9" style="display: inline;"><span class="versenum">9</span>    He puts his hand on the flint; He overturns the mountains at the roots.    </span>          <span class="versetext" id="job28-10" style="display: inline;"><span class="versenum">10</span>    He cuts out channels in the rocks, And his eye sees every precious thing.    </span>          <span class="versetext" id="job28-11" style="display: inline;"><span class="versenum">11</span>    He dams up the streams from trickling; What is hidden he brings forth to light.    </span>          <span class="versetext" id="job28-12" style="display: inline;"><span class="versenum">12</span>    "But where can wisdom be found? And where is the place of understanding?    </span>          <span class="versetext" id="job28-13" style="display: inline;"><span class="versenum">13</span>    Man does not know its value, Nor is it found in the land of the living.    </span>          <span class="versetext" id="job28-14" style="display: inline;"><span class="versenum">14</span>    The deep says, 'It is not in me'; And the sea says, 'It is not with me.'    </span>          <span class="versetext" id="job28-15" style="display: inline;"><span class="versenum">15</span>    It cannot be purchased for gold, Nor can silver be weighed for its price.    </span>          <span class="versetext" id="job28-16" style="display: inline;"><span class="versenum">16</span>    It cannot be valued in the gold of Ophir, In precious onyx or sapphire.    </span>          <span class="versetext" id="job28-17" style="display: inline;"><span class="versenum">17</span>    Neither gold nor crystal can equal it, Nor can it be exchanged for jewelry of fine gold.    </span>          <span class="versetext" id="job28-18" style="display: inline;"><span class="versenum">18</span>    No mention shall be made of coral or quartz, For the price of wisdom is above rubies.    </span>          <span class="versetext" id="job28-19" style="display: inline;"><span class="versenum">19</span>    The topaz of Ethiopia cannot equal it, Nor can it be valued in pure gold.    </span>          <span class="versetext" id="job28-20" style="display: inline;"><span class="versenum">20</span>    "From where then does wisdom come? And where is the place of understanding?    </span>          <span class="versetext" id="job28-21" style="display: inline;"><span class="versenum">21</span>    It is hidden from the eyes of all living, And concealed from the birds of the air.    </span>          <span class="versetext" id="job28-22" style="display: inline;"><span class="versenum">22</span>    Destruction and Death say, 'We have heard a report about it with our ears.'    </span>          <span class="versetext" id="job28-23" style="display: inline;"><span class="versenum">23</span>    God understands its way, And He knows its place.    </span>          <span class="versetext" id="job28-24" style="display: inline;"><span class="versenum">24</span>    For He looks to the ends of the earth, And sees under the whole heavens,    </span>          <span class="versetext" id="job28-25" style="display: inline;"><span class="versenum">25</span>    To establish a weight for the wind, And apportion the waters by measure.    </span>          <span class="versetext" id="job28-26" style="display: inline;"><span class="versenum">26</span>    When He made a law for the rain, And a path for the thunderbolt,    </span>          <span class="versetext" id="job28-27" style="display: inline;"><span class="versenum">27</span>    Then He saw wisdom <a href="http://draft.blogger.com/null" name="b"></a> and declared it; He prepared it, indeed, He searched it out.    </span>          <span class="versetext" id="job28-28" style="display: inline;"><span class="versenum">28</span>    And to man He said, 'Behold, the fear of the Lord, that is wisdom, And to depart from evil is understanding.' "&nbsp;</span><br /><span class="versetext" id="job28-28" style="display: inline;"><i>Twenty Nine Job Cont... </i></span><br /><span class="versetext" id="job29-1" style="display: inline;"><span class="versenum">1</span>    Job further continued his discourse, and said:    </span>          <span class="versetext" id="job29-2" style="display: inline;"><span class="versenum">2</span>    Oh, that I were as in months past, As in the days when God watched over me;    </span>          <span class="versetext" id="job29-3" style="display: inline;"><span class="versenum">3</span>    When His lamp shone upon my head, And when by His light I walked through darkness;    </span>          <span class="versetext" id="job29-4" style="display: inline;"><span class="versenum">4</span>    Just as I was in the days of my prime, When the friendly counsel of God was over my tent;    </span>          <span class="versetext" id="job29-5" style="display: inline;"><span class="versenum">5</span>    When the Almighty was yet with me, When my children were around me;    </span>          <span class="versetext" id="job29-6" style="display: inline;"><span class="versenum">6</span>    When my steps were bathed with cream, <a href="http://draft.blogger.com/null" name="a"></a> And the rock poured out rivers of oil for me!    </span>          <span class="versetext" id="job29-7" style="display: inline;"><span class="versenum">7</span>    "When I went out to the gate by the city, When I took my seat in the open square,    </span>          <span class="versetext" id="job29-8" style="display: inline;"><span class="versenum">8</span>    The young men saw me and hid, And the aged arose and stood;    </span>          <span class="versetext" id="job29-9" style="display: inline;"><span class="versenum">9</span>    The princes refrained from talking, And put their hand on their mouth;    </span>          <span class="versetext" id="job29-10" style="display: inline;"><span class="versenum">10</span>    The voice of nobles was hushed, And their tongue stuck to the roof of their mouth.    </span>          <span class="versetext" id="job29-11" style="display: inline;"><span class="versenum">11</span>    When the ear heard, then it blessed me, And when the eye saw, then it approved me;    </span>          <span class="versetext" id="job29-12" style="display: inline;"><span class="versenum">12</span>    Because I delivered the poor who cried out, The fatherless and the one who had no helper.    </span>          <span class="versetext" id="job29-13" style="display: inline;"><span class="versenum">13</span>    The blessing of a perishing man came upon me, And I caused the widow's heart to sing for joy.    </span>          <span class="versetext" id="job29-14" style="display: inline;"><span class="versenum">14</span>    I put on righteousness, and it clothed me; My justice was like a robe and a turban.    </span>          <span class="versetext" id="job29-15" style="display: inline;"><span class="versenum">15</span>    I was eyes to the blind, And I was feet to the lame.    </span>          <span class="versetext" id="job29-16" style="display: inline;"><span class="versenum">16</span>    I was a father to the poor, And I searched out the case that I did not know.    </span>          <span class="versetext" id="job29-17" style="display: inline;"><span class="versenum">17</span>    I broke the fangs of the wicked, And plucked the victim from his teeth.    </span>          <span class="versetext" id="job29-18" style="display: inline;"><span class="versenum">18</span>    "Then I said, 'I shall die in my nest, And multiply my days as the sand.    </span>          <span class="versetext" id="job29-19" style="display: inline;"><span class="versenum">19</span>    My root is spread out to the waters, And the dew lies all night on my branch.    </span>          <span class="versetext" id="job29-20" style="display: inline;"><span class="versenum">20</span>    My glory is fresh within me, And my bow is renewed in my hand.'    </span>          <span class="versetext" id="job29-21" style="display: inline;"><span class="versenum">21</span>    "Men listened to me and waited, And kept silence for my counsel.    </span>          <span class="versetext" id="job29-22" style="display: inline;"><span class="versenum">22</span>    After my words they did not speak again, And my speech settled on them as dew.    </span>          <span class="versetext" id="job29-23" style="display: inline;"><span class="versenum">23</span>    They waited for me as for the rain, And they opened their mouth wide as for the spring rain.    </span>          <span class="versetext" id="job29-24" style="display: inline;"><span class="versenum">24</span>    If I mocked at them, they did not believe it, And the light of my countenance they did not cast down.    </span>          <span class="versetext" id="job29-25" style="display: inline;"><span class="versenum">25</span>    I chose the way for them, and sat as chief; So I dwelt as a king in the army, As one who comforts mourners.&nbsp;</span><br /><span class="versetext" id="job29-25" style="display: inline;"><i>Thirty Job Cont... </i></span><br /><span class="versetext" id="job30-1" style="display: inline;"><span class="versenum">1</span>    "But now they mock at me, men younger than I, Whose fathers I disdained to put with the dogs of my flock.    </span>          <span class="versetext" id="job30-2" style="display: inline;"><span class="versenum">2</span>    Indeed, what profit is the strength of their hands to me? Their vigor has perished.    </span>          <span class="versetext" id="job30-3" style="display: inline;"><span class="versenum">3</span>    They are gaunt from want and famine, Fleeing late to the wilderness, desolate and waste,    </span>          <span class="versetext" id="job30-4" style="display: inline;"><span class="versenum">4</span>    Who pluck mallow by the bushes, And broom tree roots for their food.    </span>          <span class="versetext" id="job30-5" style="display: inline;"><span class="versenum">5</span>    They were driven out from among men, They shouted at them as at a thief.    </span>          <span class="versetext" id="job30-6" style="display: inline;"><span class="versenum">6</span>    They had to live in the clefts of the valleys, In caves of the earth and the rocks.    </span>          <span class="versetext" id="job30-7" style="display: inline;"><span class="versenum">7</span>    Among the bushes they brayed, Under the nettles they nestled.    </span>          <span class="versetext" id="job30-8" style="display: inline;"><span class="versenum">8</span>    They were sons of fools, Yes, sons of vile men; They were scourged from the land.    </span>          <span class="versetext" id="job30-9" style="display: inline;"><span class="versenum">9</span>    "And now I am their taunting song; Yes, I am their byword.    </span>          <span class="versetext" id="job30-10" style="display: inline;"><span class="versenum">10</span>    They abhor me, they keep far from me; They do not hesitate to spit in my face.    </span>          <span class="versetext" id="job30-11" style="display: inline;"><span class="versenum">11</span>    Because He has loosed my <a href="http://draft.blogger.com/null" name="a"></a> bowstring and afflicted me, They have cast off restraint before me.    </span>          <span class="versetext" id="job30-12" style="display: inline;"><span class="versenum">12</span>    At my right hand the rabble arises; They push away my feet, And they raise against me their ways of destruction.    </span>          <span class="versetext" id="job30-13" style="display: inline;"><span class="versenum">13</span>    They break up my path, They promote my calamity; They have no helper.    </span>          <span class="versetext" id="job30-14" style="display: inline;"><span class="versenum">14</span>    They come as broad breakers; Under the ruinous storm they roll along.    </span>          <span class="versetext" id="job30-15" style="display: inline;"><span class="versenum">15</span>    Terrors are turned upon me; They pursue my honor as the wind, And my prosperity has passed like a cloud.    </span>          <span class="versetext" id="job30-16" style="display: inline;"><span class="versenum">16</span>    "And now my soul is poured out because of my plight; The days of affliction take hold of me.    </span>          <span class="versetext" id="job30-17" style="display: inline;"><span class="versenum">17</span>    My bones are pierced in me at night, And my gnawing pains take no rest.    </span>          <span class="versetext" id="job30-18" style="display: inline;"><span class="versenum">18</span>    By great force my garment is disfigured; It binds me about as the collar of my coat.    </span>          <span class="versetext" id="job30-19" style="display: inline;"><span class="versenum">19</span>    He has cast me into the mire, And I have become like dust and ashes.    </span>          <span class="versetext" id="job30-20" style="display: inline;"><span class="versenum">20</span>    "I cry out to You, but You do not answer me; I stand up, and You regard me.    </span>          <span class="versetext" id="job30-21" style="display: inline;"><span class="versenum">21</span>    But You have become cruel to me; With the strength of Your hand You oppose me.    </span>          <span class="versetext" id="job30-22" style="display: inline;"><span class="versenum">22</span>    You lift me up to the wind and cause me to ride on it; You spoil my success.    </span>          <span class="versetext" id="job30-23" style="display: inline;"><span class="versenum">23</span>    For I know that You will bring me to death, And to the house appointed for all living.    </span>          <span class="versetext" id="job30-24" style="display: inline;"><span class="versenum">24</span>    "Surely He would not stretch out His hand against a heap of ruins, If they cry out when He destroys it.    </span>          <span class="versetext" id="job30-25" style="display: inline;"><span class="versenum">25</span>    Have I not wept for him who was in trouble? Has not my soul grieved for the poor?    </span>          <span class="versetext" id="job30-26" style="display: inline;"><span class="versenum">26</span>    But when I looked for good, evil came to me; And when I waited for light, then came darkness.    </span>          <span class="versetext" id="job30-27" style="display: inline;"><span class="versenum">27</span>    My heart is in turmoil and cannot rest; Days of affliction confront me.    </span>          <span class="versetext" id="job30-28" style="display: inline;"><span class="versenum">28</span>    I go about mourning, but not in the sun; I stand up in the assembly and cry out for help.    </span>          <span class="versetext" id="job30-29" style="display: inline;"><span class="versenum">29</span>    I am a brother of jackals, And a companion of ostriches.    </span>          <span class="versetext" id="job30-30" style="display: inline;"><span class="versenum">30</span>    My skin grows black and falls from me; My bones burn with fever.    </span>          <span class="versetext" id="job30-31" style="display: inline;"><span class="versenum">31</span>    My harp is turned to mourning, And my flute to the voice of those who weep.&nbsp;</span><br /><i><span class="versetext" id="job30-31" style="display: inline;">&nbsp;Thirty One Job completes his last complaint...</span></i><br /><br /><span class="versetext" id="job31-1" style="display: inline;"><span class="versenum">1</span>    "I have made a covenant with my eyes; Why then should I look upon a young woman?    </span>          <span class="versetext" id="job31-2" style="display: inline;"><span class="versenum">2</span>    For what is the allotment of God from above, And the inheritance of the Almighty from on high?    </span>          <span class="versetext" id="job31-3" style="display: inline;"><span class="versenum">3</span>    Is it not destruction for the wicked, And disaster for the workers of iniquity?    </span>          <span class="versetext" id="job31-4" style="display: inline;"><span class="versenum">4</span>    Does He not see my ways, And count all my steps?    </span>          <span class="versetext" id="job31-5" style="display: inline;"><span class="versenum">5</span>    "If I have walked with falsehood, Or if my foot has hastened to deceit,    </span>          <span class="versetext" id="job31-6" style="display: inline;"><span class="versenum">6</span>    Let me be weighed on honest scales, That God may know my integrity.    </span>          <span class="versetext" id="job31-7" style="display: inline;"><span class="versenum">7</span>    If my step has turned from the way, Or my heart walked after my eyes, Or if any spot adheres to my hands,    </span>          <span class="versetext" id="job31-8" style="display: inline;"><span class="versenum">8</span>    Then let me sow, and another eat; Yes, let my harvest be rooted out.    </span>          <span class="versetext" id="job31-9" style="display: inline;"><span class="versenum">9</span>    "If my heart has been enticed by a woman, Or if I have lurked at my neighbor's door,    </span>          <span class="versetext" id="job31-10" style="display: inline;"><span class="versenum">10</span>    Then let my wife grind for another, And let others bow down over her.    </span>          <span class="versetext" id="job31-11" style="display: inline;"><span class="versenum">11</span>    For that would be wickedness; Yes, it would be iniquity deserving of judgment.    </span>          <span class="versetext" id="job31-12" style="display: inline;"><span class="versenum">12</span>    For that would be a fire that consumes to destruction, And would root out all my increase.    </span>          <span class="versetext" id="job31-13" style="display: inline;"><span class="versenum">13</span>    "If I have despised the cause of my male or female servant When they complained against me,    </span>          <span class="versetext" id="job31-14" style="display: inline;"><span class="versenum">14</span>    What then shall I do when God rises up? When He punishes, how shall I answer Him?    </span>          <span class="versetext" id="job31-15" style="display: inline;"><span class="versenum">15</span>    Did not He who made me in the womb make them? Did not the same One fashion us in the womb?    </span>          <span class="versetext" id="job31-16" style="display: inline;"><span class="versenum">16</span>    "If I have kept the poor from their desire, Or caused the eyes of the widow to fail,    </span>          <span class="versetext" id="job31-17" style="display: inline;"><span class="versenum">17</span>    Or eaten my morsel by myself, So that the fatherless could not eat of it    </span>          <span class="versetext" id="job31-18" style="display: inline;"><span class="versenum">18</span>    (But from my youth I reared him as a father, And from my mother's womb I guided the widow);    </span>          <span class="versetext" id="job31-19" style="display: inline;"><span class="versenum">19</span>    If I have seen anyone perish for lack of clothing, Or any poor man without covering;    </span>          <span class="versetext" id="job31-20" style="display: inline;"><span class="versenum">20</span>    If his heart <a href="http://draft.blogger.com/null" name="a"></a> has not blessed me, And if he was not warmed with the fleece of my sheep;    </span>          <span class="versetext" id="job31-21" style="display: inline;"><span class="versenum">21</span>    If I have raised my hand against the fatherless, When I saw I had help in the gate;    </span>          <span class="versetext" id="job31-22" style="display: inline;"><span class="versenum">22</span>    Then let my arm fall from my shoulder, Let my arm be torn from the socket.    </span>          <span class="versetext" id="job31-23" style="display: inline;"><span class="versenum">23</span>    For destruction from God is a terror to me, And because of His magnificence I cannot endure.    </span>          <span class="versetext" id="job31-24" style="display: inline;"><span class="versenum">24</span>    "If I have made gold my hope, Or said to fine gold, 'You are my confidence';    </span>          <span class="versetext" id="job31-25" style="display: inline;"><span class="versenum">25</span>    If I have rejoiced because my wealth was great, And because my hand had gained much;    </span>          <span class="versetext" id="job31-26" style="display: inline;"><span class="versenum">26</span>    If I have observed the sun <a href="http://draft.blogger.com/null" name="b"></a> when it shines, Or the moon moving in brightness,    </span>          <span class="versetext" id="job31-27" style="display: inline;"><span class="versenum">27</span>    So that my heart has been secretly enticed, And my mouth has kissed my hand;    </span>          <span class="versetext" id="job31-28" style="display: inline;"><span class="versenum">28</span>    This also would be an iniquity deserving of judgment, For I would have denied God who is above.    </span>          <span class="versetext" id="job31-29" style="display: inline;"><span class="versenum">29</span>    "If I have rejoiced at the destruction of him who hated me, Or lifted myself up when evil found him    </span>          <span class="versetext" id="job31-30" style="display: inline;"><span class="versenum">30</span>    (Indeed I have not allowed my mouth to sin By asking for a curse on his soul);    </span>          <span class="versetext" id="job31-31" style="display: inline;"><span class="versenum">31</span>    If the men of my tent have not said, 'Who is there that has not been satisfied with his meat?'    </span>          <span class="versetext" id="job31-32" style="display: inline;"><span class="versenum">32</span>    (But no sojourner had to lodge in the street, For I have opened my doors to the traveler);    </span>          <span class="versetext" id="job31-33" style="display: inline;"><span class="versenum">33</span>    If I have covered my transgressions as Adam, By hiding my iniquity in my bosom,    </span>          <span class="versetext" id="job31-34" style="display: inline;"><span class="versenum">34</span>    Because I feared the great multitude, And dreaded the contempt of  families, So that I kept silence And did not go out of the door--    </span>          <span class="versetext" id="job31-35" style="display: inline;"><span class="versenum">35</span>    Oh, that I had one to hear me! Here is my mark. Oh, that the  Almighty would answer me, That my Prosecutor had written a book!    </span>          <span class="versetext" id="job31-36" style="display: inline;"><span class="versenum">36</span>    Surely I would carry it on my shoulder, And bind it on me like a crown;    </span>          <span class="versetext" id="job31-37" style="display: inline;"><span class="versenum">37</span>    I would declare to Him the number of my steps; Like a prince I would approach Him.    </span>          <span class="versetext" id="job31-38" style="display: inline;"><span class="versenum">38</span>    "If my land cries out against me, And its furrows weep together;    </span>          <span class="versetext" id="job31-39" style="display: inline;"><span class="versenum">39</span>    If I have eaten its fruit <a href="http://draft.blogger.com/null" name="c"></a> without money, Or caused its owners to lose their lives;    </span>          <span class="versetext" id="job31-40" style="display: inline;"><span class="versenum">40</span>    Then let thistles grow instead of wheat, And weeds instead of barley."   The words of Job are ended.</span><br /><br /><b><i><span class="versetext" id="job30-31" style="display: inline;">Forty Two JOB ANSWERS GOD!</span></i></b><br /><br /><span class="versetext" id="job42-1" style="display: inline;"><span class="versenum">1</span>    Then Job answered the Lord and said:    </span>          <span class="versetext" id="job42-2" style="display: inline;"><span class="versenum">2</span>    "I know that You can do everything, And that no purpose of Yours can be withheld from You.    </span>          <span class="versetext" id="job42-3" style="display: inline;"><span class="versenum">3</span>    You asked, 'Who is this who hides counsel without knowledge?'  Therefore I have uttered what I did not understand, Things too wonderful  for me, which I did not know.    </span>          <span class="versetext" id="job42-4" style="display: inline;"><span class="versenum">4</span>    Listen, please, and let me speak; You said, 'I will question you, and you shall answer Me.'    </span>          <span class="versetext" id="job42-5" style="display: inline;"><span class="versenum">5</span>    "I have heard of You by the hearing of the ear, But now my eye sees You.    </span>          <span class="versetext" id="job42-6" style="display: inline;"><span class="versenum">6</span>    Therefore I abhor myself, And repent in dust and ashes."&nbsp;</span><span class="versetext" id="job42-17" style="display: inline;"></span><br /><br /><h4 style="text-align: center;"><b>Eliphaz's Speeches</b></h4><i><span class="versetext" id="job4-1" style="display: inline;"><span class="versenum">Four</span></span></i><br /><span class="versetext" id="job4-1" style="display: inline;"><span class="versenum">1</span>    Then Eliphaz the Temanite answered and said:    </span>          <span class="versetext" id="job4-2" style="display: inline;"><span class="versenum">2</span>    "If one attempts a word with you, will you become weary? But who can withhold himself from speaking?    </span>          <span class="versetext" id="job4-3" style="display: inline;"><span class="versenum">3</span>    Surely you have instructed many, And you have strengthened weak hands.    </span>          <span class="versetext" id="job4-4" style="display: inline;"><span class="versenum">4</span>    Your words have upheld him who was stumbling, And you have strengthened the feeble knees;    </span>          <span class="versetext" id="job4-5" style="display: inline;"><span class="versenum">5</span>    But now it comes upon you, and you are weary; It touches you, and you are troubled.    </span>          <span class="versetext" id="job4-6" style="display: inline;"><span class="versenum">6</span>    Is not your reverence your confidence? And the integrity of your ways your hope?    </span>          <span class="versetext" id="job4-7" style="display: inline;"><span class="versenum">7</span>    "Remember now, who ever perished being innocent? Or where were the upright ever cut off?    </span>          <span class="versetext" id="job4-8" style="display: inline;"><span class="versenum">8</span>    Even as I have seen, Those who plow iniquity And sow trouble reap the same.    </span>          <span class="versetext" id="job4-9" style="display: inline;"><span class="versenum">9</span>    By the blast of God they perish, And by the breath of His anger they are consumed.    </span>          <span class="versetext" id="job4-10" style="display: inline;"><span class="versenum">10</span>    The roaring of the lion, The voice of the fierce lion, And the teeth of the young lions are broken.    </span>          <span class="versetext" id="job4-11" style="display: inline;"><span class="versenum">11</span>    The old lion perishes for lack of prey, And the cubs of the lioness are scattered.    </span>          <span class="versetext" id="job4-12" style="display: inline;"><span class="versenum">12</span>    "Now a word was secretly brought to me, And my ear received a whisper of it.    </span>          <span class="versetext" id="job4-13" style="display: inline;"><span class="versenum">13</span>    In disquieting thoughts from the visions of the night, When deep sleep falls on men,    </span>          <span class="versetext" id="job4-14" style="display: inline;"><span class="versenum">14</span>    Fear came upon me, and trembling, Which made all my bones shake.    </span>          <span class="versetext" id="job4-15" style="display: inline;"><span class="versenum">15</span>    Then a spirit passed before my face; The hair on my body stood up.    </span>          <span class="versetext" id="job4-16" style="display: inline;"><span class="versenum">16</span>    It stood still, But I could not discern its appearance. A form was  before my eyes; There was silence; Then I heard a voice saying:    </span>          <span class="versetext" id="job4-17" style="display: inline;"><span class="versenum">17</span>    'Can a mortal be more righteous than God? Can a man be more pure than his Maker?    </span>          <span class="versetext" id="job4-18" style="display: inline;"><span class="versenum">18</span>    If He puts no trust in His servants, If He charges His angels with error,    </span>          <span class="versetext" id="job4-19" style="display: inline;"><span class="versenum">19</span>    How much more those who dwell in houses of clay, Whose foundation is in the dust, Who are crushed before a moth?    </span>          <span class="versetext" id="job4-20" style="display: inline;"><span class="versenum">20</span>    They are broken in pieces from morning till evening; They perish forever, with no one regarding.    </span>          <span class="versetext" id="job4-21" style="display: inline;"><span class="versenum">21</span>    Does not their own excellence go away? They die, even without wisdom.'    </span><br /><i>Five</i><br /><span class="versetext" id="job5-1" style="display: inline;"><span class="versenum">1</span>    "Call out now; Is there anyone who will answer you? And to which of the holy ones will you turn?    </span>          <span class="versetext" id="job5-2" style="display: inline;"><span class="versenum">2</span>    For wrath kills a foolish man, And envy slays a simple one.    </span>          <span class="versetext" id="job5-3" style="display: inline;"><span class="versenum">3</span>    I have seen the foolish taking root, But suddenly I cursed his dwelling place.    </span>          <span class="versetext" id="job5-4" style="display: inline;"><span class="versenum">4</span>    His sons are far from safety, They are crushed in the gate, And there is no deliverer.    </span>          <span class="versetext" id="job5-5" style="display: inline;"><span class="versenum">5</span>    Because the hungry eat up his harvest, Taking it even from the thorns, <a href="http://draft.blogger.com/null" name="a"></a> And a snare snatches their substance. <a href="http://draft.blogger.com/null" name="b"></a>   </span>          <span class="versetext" id="job5-6" style="display: inline;"><span class="versenum">6</span>    For affliction does not come from the dust, Nor does trouble spring from the ground;    </span>          <span class="versetext" id="job5-7" style="display: inline;"><span class="versenum">7</span>    Yet man is born to trouble, As the sparks fly upward.    </span>          <span class="versetext" id="job5-8" style="display: inline;"><span class="versenum">8</span>    "But as for me, I would seek God, And to God I would commit my cause--    </span>          <span class="versetext" id="job5-9" style="display: inline;"><span class="versenum">9</span>    Who does great things, and unsearchable, Marvelous things without number.    </span>          <span class="versetext" id="job5-10" style="display: inline;"><span class="versenum">10</span>    He gives rain on the earth, And sends waters on the fields.    </span>          <span class="versetext" id="job5-11" style="display: inline;"><span class="versenum">11</span>    He sets on high those who are lowly, And those who mourn are lifted to safety.    </span>          <span class="versetext" id="job5-12" style="display: inline;"><span class="versenum">12</span>    He frustrates the devices of the crafty, So that their hands cannot carry out their plans.    </span>          <span class="versetext" id="job5-13" style="display: inline;"><span class="versenum">13</span>    He catches the wise in their own craftiness, And the counsel of the cunning comes quickly upon them.    </span>          <span class="versetext" id="job5-14" style="display: inline;"><span class="versenum">14</span>    They meet with darkness in the daytime, And grope at noontime as in the night.    </span>          <span class="versetext" id="job5-15" style="display: inline;"><span class="versenum">15</span>    But He saves the needy from the sword, From the mouth of the mighty, And from their hand.    </span>          <span class="versetext" id="job5-16" style="display: inline;"><span class="versenum">16</span>    So the poor have hope, And injustice shuts her mouth.    </span>          <span class="versetext" id="job5-17" style="display: inline;"><span class="versenum">17</span>    "Behold, happy is the man whom God corrects; Therefore do not despise the chastening of the Almighty.    </span>          <span class="versetext" id="job5-18" style="display: inline;"><span class="versenum">18</span>    For He bruises, but He binds up; He wounds, but His hands make whole.    </span>          <span class="versetext" id="job5-19" style="display: inline;"><span class="versenum">19</span>    He shall deliver you in six troubles, Yes, in seven no evil shall touch you.    </span>          <span class="versetext" id="job5-20" style="display: inline;"><span class="versenum">20</span>    In famine He shall redeem you from death, And in war from the power of the sword.    </span>          <span class="versetext" id="job5-21" style="display: inline;"><span class="versenum">21</span>    You shall be hidden from the scourge of the tongue, And you shall not be afraid of destruction when it comes.    </span>          <span class="versetext" id="job5-22" style="display: inline;"><span class="versenum">22</span>    You shall laugh at destruction and famine, And you shall not be afraid of the beasts of the earth.    </span>          <span class="versetext" id="job5-23" style="display: inline;"><span class="versenum">23</span>    For you shall have a covenant with the stones of the field, And the beasts of the field shall be at peace with you.    </span>          <span class="versetext" id="job5-24" style="display: inline;"><span class="versenum">24</span>    You shall know that your tent is in peace; You shall visit your dwelling and find nothing amiss.    </span>          <span class="versetext" id="job5-25" style="display: inline;"><span class="versenum">25</span>    You shall also know that your descendants shall be many, And your offspring like the grass of the earth.    </span>          <span class="versetext" id="job5-26" style="display: inline;"><span class="versenum">26</span>    You shall come to the grave at a full age, As a sheaf of grain ripens in its season.    </span>          <span class="versetext" id="job5-27" style="display: inline;"><span class="versenum">27</span>    Behold, this we have searched out; It is true. Hear it, and know for yourself."    </span><br /><i>Fifteen Eliphaz Speaks Again</i><br /><span class="versetext" id="job15-1" style="display: inline;"><span class="versenum">1</span>    Then Eliphaz the Temanite answered and said:    </span>          <span class="versetext" id="job15-2" style="display: inline;"><span class="versenum">2</span>    "Should a wise man answer with empty knowledge, And fill himself with the east wind?    </span>          <span class="versetext" id="job15-3" style="display: inline;"><span class="versenum">3</span>    Should he reason with unprofitable talk, Or by speeches with which he can do no good?    </span>          <span class="versetext" id="job15-4" style="display: inline;"><span class="versenum">4</span>    Yes, you cast off fear, And restrain prayer before God.    </span>          <span class="versetext" id="job15-5" style="display: inline;"><span class="versenum">5</span>    For your iniquity teaches your mouth, And you choose the tongue of the crafty.    </span>          <span class="versetext" id="job15-6" style="display: inline;"><span class="versenum">6</span>    Your own mouth condemns you, and not I; Yes, your own lips testify against you.    </span>          <span class="versetext" id="job15-7" style="display: inline;"><span class="versenum">7</span>    "Are you the first man who was born? Or were you made before the hills?    </span>          <span class="versetext" id="job15-8" style="display: inline;"><span class="versenum">8</span>    Have you heard the counsel of God? Do you limit wisdom to yourself?    </span>          <span class="versetext" id="job15-9" style="display: inline;"><span class="versenum">9</span>    What do you know that we do not know? What do you understand that is not in us?    </span>          <span class="versetext" id="job15-10" style="display: inline;"><span class="versenum">10</span>    Both the gray-haired and the aged are among us, Much older than your father.    </span>          <span class="versetext" id="job15-11" style="display: inline;"><span class="versenum">11</span>    Are the consolations of God too small for you, And the word spoken gently <a href="http://draft.blogger.com/null" name="a"></a> with you?    </span>          <span class="versetext" id="job15-12" style="display: inline;"><span class="versenum">12</span>    Why does your heart carry you away, And what do your eyes wink at,    </span>          <span class="versetext" id="job15-13" style="display: inline;"><span class="versenum">13</span>    That you turn your spirit against God, And let such words go out of your mouth?    </span>          <span class="versetext" id="job15-14" style="display: inline;"><span class="versenum">14</span>    "What is man, that he could be pure? And he who is born of a woman, that he could be righteous?    </span>          <span class="versetext" id="job15-15" style="display: inline;"><span class="versenum">15</span>    If God puts no trust in His saints, And the heavens are not pure in His sight,    </span>          <span class="versetext" id="job15-16" style="display: inline;"><span class="versenum">16</span>    How much less man, who is abominable and filthy, Who drinks iniquity like water!    </span>          <span class="versetext" id="job15-17" style="display: inline;"><span class="versenum">17</span>    "I will tell you, hear me; What I have seen I will declare,    </span>          <span class="versetext" id="job15-18" style="display: inline;"><span class="versenum">18</span>    What wise men have told, Not hiding anything received from their fathers,    </span>          <span class="versetext" id="job15-19" style="display: inline;"><span class="versenum">19</span>    To whom alone the land was given, And no alien passed among them:    </span>          <span class="versetext" id="job15-20" style="display: inline;"><span class="versenum">20</span>    The wicked man writhes with pain all his days, And the number of years is hidden from the oppressor.    </span>          <span class="versetext" id="job15-21" style="display: inline;"><span class="versenum">21</span>    Dreadful sounds are in his ears; In prosperity the destroyer comes upon him.    </span>          <span class="versetext" id="job15-22" style="display: inline;"><span class="versenum">22</span>    He does not believe that he will return from darkness, For a sword is waiting for him.    </span>          <span class="versetext" id="job15-23" style="display: inline;"><span class="versenum">23</span>    He wanders about for bread, saying, 'Where is it?' He knows that a day of darkness is ready at his hand.    </span>          <span class="versetext" id="job15-24" style="display: inline;"><span class="versenum">24</span>    Trouble and anguish make him afraid; They overpower him, like a king ready for battle.    </span>          <span class="versetext" id="job15-25" style="display: inline;"><span class="versenum">25</span>    For he stretches out his hand against God, And acts defiantly against the Almighty,    </span>          <span class="versetext" id="job15-26" style="display: inline;"><span class="versenum">26</span>    Running stubbornly against Him With his strong, embossed shield.    </span>          <span class="versetext" id="job15-27" style="display: inline;"><span class="versenum">27</span>    "Though he has covered his face with his fatness, And made his waist heavy with fat,    </span>          <span class="versetext" id="job15-28" style="display: inline;"><span class="versenum">28</span>    He dwells in desolate cities, In houses which no one inhabits, Which are destined to become ruins.    </span>          <span class="versetext" id="job15-29" style="display: inline;"><span class="versenum">29</span>    He will not be rich, Nor will his wealth continue, Nor will his possessions overspread the earth.    </span>          <span class="versetext" id="job15-30" style="display: inline;"><span class="versenum">30</span>    He will not depart from darkness; The flame will dry out his branches, And by the breath of His mouth he will go away.    </span>          <span class="versetext" id="job15-31" style="display: inline;"><span class="versenum">31</span>    Let him not trust in futile things, deceiving himself, For futility will be his reward.    </span>          <span class="versetext" id="job15-32" style="display: inline;"><span class="versenum">32</span>    It will be accomplished before his time, And his branch will not be green.    </span>          <span class="versetext" id="job15-33" style="display: inline;"><span class="versenum">33</span>    He will shake off his unripe grape like a vine, And cast off his blossom like an olive tree.    </span>          <span class="versetext" id="job15-34" style="display: inline;"><span class="versenum">34</span>    For the company of hypocrites will be barren, And fire will consume the tents of bribery.    </span>          <span class="versetext" id="job15-35" style="display: inline;"><span class="versenum">35</span>    They conceive trouble and bring forth futility; Their womb prepares deceit."&nbsp;</span><br /><span class="versetext" id="job15-35" style="display: inline;">&nbsp;<i>Twenty Two Eliphaz Third Speech</i></span><br /><br /><span class="versetext" id="job22-1" style="display: inline;"><span class="versenum">1</span>    Then Eliphaz the Temanite answered and said:    </span>          <span class="versetext" id="job22-2" style="display: inline;"><span class="versenum">2</span>    "Can a man be profitable to God, Though he who is wise may be profitable to himself?    </span>          <span class="versetext" id="job22-3" style="display: inline;"><span class="versenum">3</span>    Is it any pleasure to the Almighty that you are righteous? Or is it gain to Him that you make your ways blameless?    </span>          <span class="versetext" id="job22-4" style="display: inline;"><span class="versenum">4</span>    "Is it because of your fear of Him that He corrects you, And enters into judgment with you?    </span>          <span class="versetext" id="job22-5" style="display: inline;"><span class="versenum">5</span>    Is not your wickedness great, And your iniquity without end?    </span>          <span class="versetext" id="job22-6" style="display: inline;"><span class="versenum">6</span>    For you have taken pledges from your brother for no reason, And stripped the naked of their clothing.    </span>          <span class="versetext" id="job22-7" style="display: inline;"><span class="versenum">7</span>    You have not given the weary water to drink, And you have withheld bread from the hungry.    </span>          <span class="versetext" id="job22-8" style="display: inline;"><span class="versenum">8</span>    But the mighty man possessed the land, And the honorable man dwelt in it.    </span>          <span class="versetext" id="job22-9" style="display: inline;"><span class="versenum">9</span>    You have sent widows away empty, And the strength of the fatherless was crushed.    </span>          <span class="versetext" id="job22-10" style="display: inline;"><span class="versenum">10</span>    Therefore snares are all around you, And sudden fear troubles you,    </span>          <span class="versetext" id="job22-11" style="display: inline;"><span class="versenum">11</span>    Or darkness so that you cannot see; And an abundance of water covers you.    </span>          <span class="versetext" id="job22-12" style="display: inline;"><span class="versenum">12</span>    "Is not God in the height of heaven? And see the highest stars, how lofty they are!    </span>          <span class="versetext" id="job22-13" style="display: inline;"><span class="versenum">13</span>    And you say, 'What does God know? Can He judge through the deep darkness?    </span>          <span class="versetext" id="job22-14" style="display: inline;"><span class="versenum">14</span>    Thick clouds cover Him, so that He cannot see, And He walks above the circle of heaven.'    </span>          <span class="versetext" id="job22-15" style="display: inline;"><span class="versenum">15</span>    Will you keep to the old way Which wicked men have trod,    </span>          <span class="versetext" id="job22-16" style="display: inline;"><span class="versenum">16</span>    Who were cut down before their time, Whose foundations were swept away by a flood?    </span>          <span class="versetext" id="job22-17" style="display: inline;"><span class="versenum">17</span>    They said to God, 'Depart from us! What can the Almighty do to them?' <a href="http://draft.blogger.com/null" name="a"></a>   </span>          <span class="versetext" id="job22-18" style="display: inline;"><span class="versenum">18</span>    Yet He filled their houses with good things; But the counsel of the wicked is far from me.    </span>          <span class="versetext" id="job22-19" style="display: inline;"><span class="versenum">19</span>    "The righteous see it and are glad, And the innocent laugh at them:    </span>          <span class="versetext" id="job22-20" style="display: inline;"><span class="versenum">20</span>    'Surely our adversaries <a href="http://draft.blogger.com/null" name="b"></a> are cut down, And the fire consumes their remnant.'    </span>          <span class="versetext" id="job22-21" style="display: inline;"><span class="versenum">21</span>    "Now acquaint yourself with Him, and be at peace; Thereby good will come to you.    </span>          <span class="versetext" id="job22-22" style="display: inline;"><span class="versenum">22</span>    Receive, please, instruction from His mouth, And lay up His words in your heart.    </span>          <span class="versetext" id="job22-23" style="display: inline;"><span class="versenum">23</span>    If you return to the Almighty, you will be built up; You will remove iniquity far from your tents.    </span>          <span class="versetext" id="job22-24" style="display: inline;"><span class="versenum">24</span>    Then you will lay your gold in the dust, And the gold of Ophir among the stones of the brooks.    </span>          <span class="versetext" id="job22-25" style="display: inline;"><span class="versenum">25</span>    Yes, the Almighty will be your gold <a href="http://draft.blogger.com/null" name="c"></a> And your precious silver;    </span>          <span class="versetext" id="job22-26" style="display: inline;"><span class="versenum">26</span>    For then you will have your delight in the Almighty, And lift up your face to God.    </span>          <span class="versetext" id="job22-27" style="display: inline;"><span class="versenum">27</span>    You will make your prayer to Him, He will hear you, And you will pay your vows.    </span>          <span class="versetext" id="job22-28" style="display: inline;"><span class="versenum">28</span>    You will also declare a thing, And it will be established for you; So light will shine on your ways.    </span>          <span class="versetext" id="job22-29" style="display: inline;"><span class="versenum">29</span>    When they cast you down, and you say, 'Exaltation will come!' Then He will save the humble person.    </span>          <span class="versetext" id="job22-30" style="display: inline;"><span class="versenum">30</span>    He will even deliver one who is not innocent; Yes, he will be delivered by the purity of your hands."    </span><br /><br /><h4 style="text-align: center;"><b>Bildad</b>'s Speeches</h4>Eight<br /><span class="versetext" id="job8-1" style="display: inline;"><span class="versenum">1</span>    Then Bildad the Shuhite answered and said:    </span>          <span class="versetext" id="job8-2" style="display: inline;"><span class="versenum">2</span>    "How long will you speak these things, And the words of your mouth be like a strong wind?    </span>          <span class="versetext" id="job8-3" style="display: inline;"><span class="versenum">3</span>    Does God subvert judgment? Or does the Almighty pervert justice?    </span>          <span class="versetext" id="job8-4" style="display: inline;"><span class="versenum">4</span>    If your sons have sinned against Him, He has cast them away for their transgression.    </span>          <span class="versetext" id="job8-5" style="display: inline;"><span class="versenum">5</span>    If you would earnestly seek God And make your supplication to the Almighty,    </span>          <span class="versetext" id="job8-6" style="display: inline;"><span class="versenum">6</span>    If you were pure and upright, Surely now He would awake for you, And prosper your rightful dwelling place.    </span>          <span class="versetext" id="job8-7" style="display: inline;"><span class="versenum">7</span>    Though your beginning was small, Yet your latter end would increase abundantly.    </span>          <span class="versetext" id="job8-8" style="display: inline;"><span class="versenum">8</span>    "For inquire, please, of the former age, And consider the things discovered by their fathers;    </span>          <span class="versetext" id="job8-9" style="display: inline;"><span class="versenum">9</span>    For we were born yesterday, and know nothing, Because our days on earth are a shadow.    </span>          <span class="versetext" id="job8-10" style="display: inline;"><span class="versenum">10</span>    Will they not teach you and tell you, And utter words from their heart?    </span>          <span class="versetext" id="job8-11" style="display: inline;"><span class="versenum">11</span>    "Can the papyrus grow up without a marsh? Can the reeds flourish without water?    </span>          <span class="versetext" id="job8-12" style="display: inline;"><span class="versenum">12</span>    While it is yet green and not cut down, It withers before any other plant.    </span>          <span class="versetext" id="job8-13" style="display: inline;"><span class="versenum">13</span>    So are the paths of all who forget God; And the hope of the hypocrite shall perish,    </span>          <span class="versetext" id="job8-14" style="display: inline;"><span class="versenum">14</span>    Whose confidence shall be cut off, And whose trust is a spider's web.    </span>          <span class="versetext" id="job8-15" style="display: inline;"><span class="versenum">15</span>    He leans on his house, but it does not stand.
